Container.Name: formulaar1
Container.Overview:
Formulaar1 sits between AutoBrr and Sonarr to translate releases with non-standard episode numbering into the correct TVDB episode (via an external round/circuit lookup API) and forward to Sonarr with the correct metadata.
Pipeline: AutoBrr --> Formulaar1 --> Sonarr
Unofficial Docker packaging of Jimmy062006/Formulaar1. See https://github.com/Healzangels/formulaar1-docker for source and the sample appsettings.json.
SETUP: before starting, place a configured appsettings.json at /mnt/user/appdata/formulaar1/appsettings.json (copy appsettings.example.json from the repo and fill in Sonarr API key, qBittorrent creds, and Hardlinkpath).
Then in AutoBrr add a Sonarr-type client pointing at http://<this-host>:5000 with your NORMAL Sonarr API key.
Container.Description: Formulaar1 sits between AutoBrr and Sonarr to translate releases with non-standard episode numbering into the correct TVDB episode and forward to Sonarr with the correct metadata.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port Formulaar1 listens on. AutoBrr's Sonarr-type client points at http://<unraid-ip>:<this port>.

Config.Name: appsettings.json
Config.Description: Bind-mounts your configured appsettings.json into the container. Create this file BEFORE starting the container -- copy appsettings.example.json from the GitHub repo and fill in Sonarr API key, qBittorrent creds, and Hardlinkpath.

Config.Name: Data Share
Config.Description: MUST match the path mapping used by qBittorrent and Sonarr so hardlinks work. /data inside the container should resolve to the same files on the host that the rest of your *arr stack sees.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID -- 99 (nobody) is the Unraid default.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID -- 100 (users) is the Unraid default.
